$comment: DRAFT interaction suite for the done-loop (P1). Review target: ~8-12 interactions across two classes. 'modify' = tool-calling (issues create_request -> CODAP), judged on document-state deltas. 'describe' = non-tool (DAVAI answers from the dataContexts/graphs embedded in its system prompt), judged by LLM-judge semantic equivalence vs the old backend. Fixture is CODAP's built-in Mammals sample (load via `?sample=mammals&dashboard`), a stable known dataset.

fixture.name: mammals
fixture.note: 27 cases in the standard sample; the latency/parity runner should snapshot the exact case/attribute counts at run time rather than hard-coding them.

interactions.prompt: Make a scatterplot of Height versus Mass.
Show me a dot plot of LifeSpan.
Make a scatterplot of Height versus Mass, colored by Diet.
Select all the mammals that live in the water.
Add a new attribute called SpeedPerMass that is Speed divided by Mass.
Group the mammals by their Order.
How many mammals and how many attributes are in this dataset?
What attributes does this dataset have, and what type is each one?

mustState: identifies numeric vs categorical roughly correctly

interactions.prompt: What is the average lifespan of these mammals?

mustState: a mean LifeSpan value within a small tolerance of the true mean

interactions.prompt: Describe the distribution of Mass. Is it skewed?
Is there a relationship between a mammal's Height and its Mass?
